title: Living in Des Peres, MO
content:
Des Peres is a West St. Louis County suburb approximately 25 minutes from downtown, positioned where inner-ring suburbs transition to more spread-out suburbia. I-270 bisects the community (population 9,145).

UNIQUE FEATURES:
- No Property Tax: Des Peres pioneered a distinctive funding model over 30 years ago, taxing online transactions instead of collecting property taxes.
- Shopping Hub: West County Center mall and numerous retailers along Manchester Road.

HOUSING MARKET:
- Median selling price: $685,000 (2025)
- Median days on market: 26 days
- Per-square-foot pricing: $260-$280; luxury exceeds $300
- Primarily single-family homes from 1950s-1980s plus newer construction

NEIGHBORHOODS:
- Manhattan Heights: Mid-century ranches, entry-level around $400,000s
- Harwood Hills & Berkley Manor: Upscale, larger lots, high-end properties
- Royal Acres: 1970s development, 10+ acres common grounds, community pool
- Dougherty Lake: Late 1970s-early 1980s, private lake, walking trails
- Enclave at Greenbriar: Premier new community of 19 townhomes (3,000-4,200+ sq ft)

DEMOGRAPHICS:
- ~3,220 households, avg household size 2.8
- Median household income: $214,167
- 76% of adults hold bachelor's degree or higher
- 22% retirees

EDUCATION:
- Split between Parkway Schools (west) and Kirkwood Schools (east)
- Kirkwood High School ranks 5th best public high school in Missouri
- Private options: CBC, Principia School, Whitfield School

PARKS & RECREATION:
- The Lodge: full gym, indoor walking track, indoor pool, fitness classes, seasonal water park
- Six parks totaling ~100 acres

HEALTHCARE:
- Mercy Hospital (10 min), Barnes-Jewish West County, Missouri Baptist Medical Center

EVENTS:
- Summer Concert Series, Fall Fest, Holiday Lights Contest, Bonfire and hayrides
- The whimsical Des Peres Pickle Jar on a highway guardrail

Walk Score: 71, Bike Score: 53. Convenient I-270 access to Forest Park, Lambert Airport, Grant's Farm, Gateway Arch.
